name: Nan (Fried Leavened Bread)
recipeInstructions:
1. Warm the yogurt and stir in milk until thoroughly mixed.
2. Remove from heat.
3. Add baking soda, sugar, 4 tablespoons butter/Oleo, eggs, and dry yeast to the yogurt mixture.
4. Sift together the flour and salt. Make a well in the flour and gradually add the yogurt mixture.
5. Knead the dough for 15 to 20 minutes until smooth and elastic.
6. Brush the dough with some of the remaining butter/Oleo. Cover with a warm, damp cloth and set aside in a warm place for 3 hours until the dough has risen to twice its size.
7. Dust hands with flour. Knead the dough again for a few minutes and divide into 8 balls.
8. Roll each ball into a 10-inch pancake. Gently pull each pancake to give it an oval shape.
9. Cover with damp cloth for 20 minutes.
10. Heat a griddle until very hot. Mix the remaining 4 tablespoons butter/Oleo with poppy seed.
11. Brush one side of each nan with the mixture and the other side with warm water.
12. Place the warm-water side on the griddle for 1/2 minute. Remove from the griddle and place nan under broiler for about 2 minutes.
13. Serve with curries and tandoori dishes.
